description: Adopt React Compiler for the browser extension side panel. Integrate babel-plugin-react-compiler into the esbuild and Storybook build pipelines, upgrade ESLint to use the compiler's recommended-latest rules, remove all manual useCallback/useMemo/React.memo that the compiler now handles automatically, and update CLAUDE.md to reflect the new conventions.

userStories.title: Integrate babel-plugin-react-compiler into the esbuild side panel build
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the side panel esbuild build to run React Compiler so that all React components and hooks are automatically memoized at build time.
acceptanceCriteria: babel-plugin-react-compiler and @babel/core are added as devDependencies in root package.json
esbuild-plugin-babel is added as a devDependency in root package.json
platform/browser-extension/build-side-panel.ts uses an esbuild-plugin-babel plugin configured with babel-plugin-react-compiler as the first (and only) Babel plugin
The Babel plugin is configured to only process .tsx and .ts files (via a filter regex)

The esbuild plugin is configured to use @babel/preset-typescript for TypeScript parsing, since esbuild handles TSX->JS first but the compiler needs the original JSX/TS source — alternatively, if esbuild-plugin-babel runs Babel before esbuild's own transform, ensure the plugin pipeline is: babel-plugin-react-compiler first, then @babel/preset-typescript, then @babel/preset-react with runtime 'automatic'
The built side-panel.js output contains react/compiler-runtime imports (evidence the compiler ran)
userStories.notes: The side panel is bundled by esbuild in platform/browser-extension/build-side-panel.ts. It currently bundles src/side-panel/index.tsx with no Babel step. To add React Compiler, install esbuild-plugin-babel (npm package, wraps Babel transforms into an esbuild plugin), @babel/core, @babel/preset-typescript, @babel/preset-react, and babel-plugin-react-compiler. In build-side-panel.ts, add the esbuild-plugin-babel plugin to the esbuild build config. The Babel plugin pipeline should be: [babel-plugin-react-compiler] first, then presets [@babel/preset-typescript, @babel/preset-react with {runtime: 'automatic'}]. Use a filter like /\.[jt]sx?$/ to only process JS/TS/JSX/TSX files. The compiler targets React 19 by default which matches our setup. After building, verify the output contains 'react/compiler-runtime' or the _c helper import. Note: esbuild-plugin-babel replaces esbuild's built-in JSX/TS handling for matched files, so the Babel presets must handle both TypeScript stripping and JSX transformation. The define for process.env.NODE_ENV should remain in the esbuild config.

userStories.title: Integrate React Compiler into Storybook Vite config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Storybook to also use React Compiler so that component stories reflect the same compilation behavior as the production build.
acceptanceCriteria: npx storybook build (or npm run storybook:build in platform/browser-extension) completes successfully
userStories.notes: The Storybook config is at platform/browser-extension/.storybook/main.ts. It uses @storybook/react-vite and already has a viteFinal hook that adds the @tailwindcss/vite plugin. @vitejs/plugin-react is already a devDependency. In viteFinal, find or add the @vitejs/plugin-react plugin with babel config: react({ babel: { plugins: ['babel-plugin-react-compiler'] } }). If the react plugin is already auto-added by @storybook/react-vite, you may need to remove the auto-added one and add a configured one. Check if viteConfig.plugins already contains the react plugin and replace it, or use the storybook framework config to pass the react plugin options. The @vitejs/plugin-react Vite plugin natively supports Babel plugins via its babel option, so no separate babel bridge is needed for Vite/Storybook.

userStories.title: Upgrade ESLint react-hooks config to recommended-latest for compiler rules
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the ESLint config to use eslint-plugin-react-hooks recommended-latest preset so that the React Compiler's lint rules (including Rules of React violations) are enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: eslint.config.ts uses plugin:react-hooks/recommended-latest instead of plugin:react-hooks/recommended
npm run lint passes with no new errors (or any new errors are fixed)
userStories.notes: In eslint.config.ts line 25, the react-hooks config is applied via FlatCompat: fixupConfigRules(new FlatCompat().extends('plugin:react-hooks/recommended')). Change 'plugin:react-hooks/recommended' to 'plugin:react-hooks/recommended-latest'. The main difference is that recommended-latest adds the void-use-memo rule. After the change, run npm run lint and fix any new violations. The recommended-latest config includes all the React Compiler lint rules that detect Rules of React violations. eslint-plugin-react-hooks@7.0.1 is already installed and includes these rules — no new dependency is needed.

userStories.title: Remove manual useCallback calls that React Compiler now handles
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove all manual useCallback wrapping from the side panel code so that the codebase relies on React Compiler's automatic memoization, reducing code complexity and cognitive overhead.
acceptanceCriteria: All useCallback imports and calls are removed from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx
All useCallback imports and calls are removed from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/hooks/useServerNotifications.ts
All useCallback imports and calls are removed from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Footer.tsx
All useCallback imports and calls are removed from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/hooks/useTheme.ts
Functions previously wrapped in useCallback are now plain function declarations or arrow function const assignments
No useMemo or React.memo exists in the codebase (verify none were added)
userStories.notes: There are 11 useCallback calls across 4 files. React Compiler automatically memoizes these, so the manual useCallback wrapping is unnecessary. For each useCallback call, unwrap it: replace `const foo = useCallback((args) => { body }, [deps])` with `const foo = (args) => { body }`. Remove the dependency arrays entirely — the compiler tracks dependencies automatically. Remove `useCallback` from import statements. If the import becomes empty (no other hooks from 'react' imported), remove the import line. Files and approximate line numbers: (1) platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx — lines 60, 100, 123, 152, 171, 267, 280 — 7 useCallback calls. (2) platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/hooks/useServerNotifications.ts — lines 51, 59 — 2 useCallback calls. (3) platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Footer.tsx — line 24 — 1 useCallback call. (4) platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/hooks/useTheme.ts — line 31 — 1 useCallback call. After removing, verify the app still builds and the E2E tests pass (this story is an e2eCheckpoint because it changes runtime behavior of the side panel).

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md to reflect React Compiler conventions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the browser extension CLAUDE.md to document React Compiler usage so that future contributors understand the memoization strategy and do not reintroduce manual useCallback/useMemo.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md React Best Practices section is updated to mention React Compiler
The section explains that React Compiler handles memoization automatically at build time

The guidance on useMemo/useCallback is updated: they should NOT be used for optimization (compiler handles it), but can still be used as escape hatches for precise control when needed (e.g., effect dependencies)
The section mentions that React.memo is also unnecessary with the compiler
The section does NOT recommend adding useCallback/useMemo for optimization purposes
userStories.notes: In platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md, the 'React Best Practices' section currently says: '- **`useMemo`/`useCallback` only when justified** — do not wrap trivial computations (array filters, string formatting) in `useMemo`. Reserve memoization for genuinely expensive calculations or when a stable reference is required (e.g., effect dependencies, context values).' Update this to reflect that React Compiler handles memoization automatically. The new guidance should state: the side panel build uses React Compiler (babel-plugin-react-compiler), which automatically memoizes all components and hooks at build time. Do not add useMemo, useCallback, or React.memo for optimization — the compiler handles this. These hooks can still be used as escape hatches when precise control over memoization is needed (e.g., ensuring an effect dependency is stable), but this should be rare. Also update the opening paragraph of the React Best Practices section to mention React Compiler. Keep the update concise — this is a CLAUDE.md, not a tutorial.
